About

Not Just a Trader.
A Market Researcher.

Shaker Mohsen is a professional forex trader with over five years of real capital exposure across major currency pairs and commodities. His edge is built on deep understanding of institutional order flow, liquidity mechanics, and the psychology behind collective market behavior — not indicators or guesswork.

As a Smart Money Concepts specialist, Shaker studies the market the way institutions operate it — through liquidity engineering, displacement, and order block validation. His methodology is structured, evidence-based, and grounded in real trade execution.

Beyond price, Shaker researches human behavior and crowd psychology — understanding why the majority consistently loses, and how disciplined, structured thinking creates sustained edge in markets.

    Liquidity & Market Structure

    Analysis framework built around where liquidity pools form, how they are engineered, and the precise moments of institutional participation that move price.

  • 04

    Human Behavior Researcher

    Study of crowd psychology, retail trader patterns, and the behavioral traps that institutional algorithms are designed to exploit systematically, week after week.

  • 05

    Weekly live sessions, structured breakdowns, and an active Arabic-speaking community built around SMC methodology and professional execution standards.

Market Philosophy
"The market moves on fear, greed, liquidity, and collective behavior —
not random indicators."

— Shaker Mohsen

01

◈

Liquidity Behavior

Markets are engineered to hunt liquidity. Stop-loss clusters, equal highs and lows, and retail pattern traps are the mechanism through which smart money creates institutional entry conditions. The crowd provides the fuel — the institution ignites it.

02

⬡

Institutional Thinking

Adopting the perspective of the participant with sufficient volume to move markets changes everything. Order flow, delivery, and displacement become the new language of price. Individual candles are replaced by intent, context, and consequence.

03

◇

Collective Psychology

Fear and greed are not random emotions — they are predictable behavioral patterns that repeat. The majority consistently loses for the same identifiable reasons. Identifying these crowd reactions in real-time defines the edge of the disciplined trader.

Methodology

The Framework.
Four Core Pillars.

Every trade passes through the same four-stage filter. Not as a checklist — as a discipline. The framework does not predict the market. It asks the right questions at the right time, and acts only when the answers align.

01

⬡

Market Structure

Reading the market's intent through swing highs, swing lows, BOS and CHoCH. Structure defines direction — everything else is confirmation. No clear structure means no trade.

02

◈

Liquidity

Identifying where stop-losses cluster, where retail positions concentrate, and how the market systematically harvests them before true directional moves begin.

03

◇

Order Blocks

The last institutional candle before significant displacement. The precise origin of smart money commitment — the most surgical entry model in the SMC framework.

04

▦

Risk Management

Mechanical position sizing, defined risk per trade, minimum 3:1 RRR. Capital preservation is the prerequisite for compounding. Non-negotiable, every trade.

Weekly Planning

Structure Before
The Market Opens.

The week begins before Monday's open. Every session is prepared — bias established from weekly and daily structure, key levels identified, kill zone windows marked. Published live to the community every Sunday.

01

Weekly Bias Analysis

HTF review of weekly & daily closes. Direction established before any intraday decision is made.

02

Key Level Identification

Premium/discount zones, OBs, liquidity pools, and structural pivots mapped across all timeframes.

03

Kill Zone Scheduling

London, NY, and Asia sessions evaluated for confluence. Only kill zone entries are considered valid.

04

Invalidation Defined

Pre-defined conditions for bias invalidation. Structure breaks — the plan adapts, not the risk.

Consistency

Discipline Is Not
Motivation. It Is System.

The edge is not in finding perfect trades. It is in executing the same structured process — repeatedly, with precision — regardless of emotion, noise, or outcome.

0:1
Minimum R:R Required
1%
Max Risk Per Trade
HTF
Bias Always Confirms
0
Impulse Trades Allowed

// 01

No Bias — No Trade

If the higher timeframe does not provide a clear directional bias, the day ends before it begins. Clarity precedes capital deployment. Always.

// 02

Repeatable Setups Only

Only setups with documented historical occurrence and structural backing are executed. Novel patterns wait in the journal — not the account.

// 03

Structured Decision Making

Every entry follows a checklist. Structure, liquidity, POI, confirmation, RRR. The process eliminates emotional override from execution, every single time.
